  3164→
  3165→**Filter Options**
  3166→
  3167→| Filter | Options | Default |
  3168→|--------|---------|---------|
  3169→| Date | Today, Tomorrow, Next 7 days, Custom | Today |
  3170→| Origin | All stations | All |
  3171→| Status | All, Unassigned, Allocated | Unassigned |
  3172→| Search | Free text on service code | Empty |
  3173→
  3174→#### 13.2 Recommendation Engine Integration
  3175→
  3176→**Request Flow**
  3177→
  3178→```
  3179→User selects service
  3180→        ↓
  3181→Frontend calls POST /api/recommend
  3182→        ↓
  3183→API forwards to Python solver
  3184→        ↓
  3185→Solver returns ranked list with justifications
  3186→        ↓
  3187→Frontend displays recommendations
  3188→```
  3189→
  3190→**Recommendation API**
  3191→
  3192→```typescript
  3193→// Request
  3194→interface RecommendRequest {
  3195→  serviceId: string;
  3196→  date: string;  // ISO date
  3197→  limit?: number; // Max recommendations (default 5)
  3198→}
  3199→
  3200→// Response
  3201→interface RecommendResponse {
  3202→  recommendations: Recommendation[];
  3203→  computeTimeMs: number;
  3204→}
  3205→
  3206→interface Recommendation {
  3207→  trainId: string;
  3208→  trainCode: string;        // UTE-3400-012
  3209→  score: number;            // 0-100
  3210→  justifications: Justification[];
  3211→  warnings?: string[];
  3212→}
  3213→
  3214→interface Justification {
  3215→  type: 'km_balance' | 'depot_proximity' | 'maintenance' | 'aptitude' | 'dead_running';
  3216→  description: string;
  3217→  impact: 'positive' | 'neutral' | 'negative';
  3218→  weight: number;           // Contribution to score
  3219→}
  3220→```
  3221→
  3222→**Solver Scoring Factors**
  3223→
  3224→| Factor | Weight | Description |
  3225→|--------|--------|-------------|
  3226→| Km Balance | 40% | Distance from fleet average |
  3227→| Depot Proximity | 25% | End position near maintenance depot |
  3228→| Maintenance Window | 20% | Alignment with scheduled maintenance |
  3229→| Dead Running | 15% | Empty km to reach service start |
  3230→
  3231→#### 13.3 Justification Display
  3232→
  3233→**Justification Card Design**
  3234→
  3235→```
  3236→┌─ Recommendation #1 ─────────────────────────────────┐
  3237→│                                                     │
  3238→│ UTE-3400-012                    Score: 94/100      │
  3239→│ Metro Series 3400 | Lisbon                         │
  3240→│                                                     │
  3241→│ ┌─ Why this train? ───────────────────────────────┐│
  3242→│ │                                                 ││
  3243→│ │ ✅ Km 12% below fleet average                   ││
  3244→│ │    Prioritizing this train helps balance fleet  ││
  3245→│ │                                                 ││
  3246→│ │ ✅ Service ends 5km from Porto depot            ││
  3247→│ │    Minimizes dead running for maintenance      ││
  3248→│ │                                                 ││
  3249→│ │ ✅ PM1 scheduled in 3 days at Porto             ││
  3250→│ │    Natural alignment with maintenance window   ││
  3251→│ │                                                 ││
  3252→│ │ ⚠️ 45km dead running to reach Lisbon start     ││
  3253→│ │    Acceptable trade-off for km balancing       ││
  3254→│ │                                                 ││
  3255→│ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
  3256→│                                                     │
  3257→│                              [Allocate This Train] │
  3258→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  3259→```
  3260→
  3261→**Justification Icons**
  3262→
  3263→| Icon | Meaning |
  3264→|------|---------|
  3265→| ✅ | Positive factor (contributes to high score) |
  3266→| ⚠️ | Trade-off or warning (not deal-breaker) |
  3267→| ❌ | Negative factor (rarely shown for recommendations) |
  3268→
  3269→**Minimum Justifications**
  3270→
  3271→Every recommendation MUST display at least 3 justifications. If the solver produces fewer, the system must generate default explanations based on the scoring factors.
  3272→
  3273→#### 13.4 One-Click Allocation
  3274→
  3275→**Allocation Flow**
  3276→
  3277→```
  3278→1. User clicks "Allocate This Train"
  3279→2. Confirmation dialog appears (skippable preference)
  3280→3. System calls POST /api/allocations
  3281→4. On success: Toast "Service IC 501 allocated to UTE-3400-012"
  3282→5. Service status updates to "allocated"
  3283→6. User sees updated service list
  3284→```
  3285→
  3286→**Allocation API**
  3287→
  3288→```typescript
  3289→// Request
  3290→interface AllocationRequest {
  3291→  serviceId: string;
  3292→  trainId: string;
  3293→  date: string;
  3294→}
  3295→
  3296→// Response
  3297→interface AllocationResponse {
  3298→  allocationId: string;
  3299→  status: 'success' | 'conflict' | 'error';
  3300→  message?: string;
  3301→}
  3302→```
  3303→
  3304→**Conflict Handling**
  3305→
  3306→If allocation fails due to conflict:
  3307→
  3308→```
  3309→┌─ Allocation Conflict ───────────────────────────────┐
  3310→│                                                     │
  3311→│ ⚠️ Cannot allocate UTE-3400-012 to IC 501          │
  3312→│                                                     │
  3313→│ Reason: Train already allocated to IC 503          │
  3314→│         (Porto → Braga, 09:00-11:30)               │
  3315→│                                                     │
  3316→│ Options:                                            │
  3317→│ • Choose a different train                          │
  3318→│ • Remove existing allocation first                  │
  3319→│                                                     │
  3320→│                    [Cancel]  [View Conflict]        │
  3321→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  3322→```
  3323→
  3324→#### 13.5 Train Filtering Criteria
  3325→
  3326→**Available Train Filters**
  3327→
  3328→```
  3329→┌─ Filter Trains ─────────────────────────────────────┐
  3330→│                                                     │
  3331→│ Fleet:    [All ▼] [Series 3400] [Series 2200]      │
  3332→│ Status:   [🔘 Fit only] [⚪ Include unfit]         │
  3333→│ Depot:    [All ▼] [Lisbon] [Porto] [Coimbra]       │
  3334→│ Km Range: [Min: ____] [Max: ____]                  │
  3335→│                                                     │
  3336→│ [Apply Filters]  [Clear All]                        │
  3337→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  3338→```
  3339→
  3340→**Filter Logic**
  3341→
  3342→- Filters are AND-combined (all conditions must match)
  3343→- Empty/default filter = include all
  3344→- Unfit trains hidden by default (can be shown with toggle)
  3345→- Results update as filters change (debounced 300ms)
  3346→

  3349→### Chapter 14: Module - Km Balancing & Simulation
  3350→
  3351→The balancing module demonstrates the core differentiator: treating km variance as a first-class concern.
  3352→
  3353→#### 14.1 Distribution Visualization (Histogram)

  3400→
  3401→#### 14.2 Train Ranking by Delta
  3402→
  3403→**Ranking Table**
  3404→
  3405→```
  3406→┌─ Train Km Rankings ─────────────────────────────────┐
  3407→│                                                     │
  3408→│ View: [Over-running ▼]  Sort: [Delta % ▼]          │
  3409→│                                                     │
  3410→│ Rank │ Train        │ Current Km │ Delta │ Status  │
  3411→│ ─────┼──────────────┼────────────┼───────┼─────────│
  3412→│  1   │ UTE-3400-025 │   52,200   │ +23%  │ 🔴 HIGH │
  3413→│  2   │ UTE-2200-018 │   50,100   │ +19%  │ 🟡 MED  │
  3414→│  3   │ UTE-3400-031 │   49,800   │ +17%  │ 🟡 MED  │
  3415→│ ...  │              │            │       │         │
  3416→│                                                     │
  3417→│ View: [Under-running ▼]                            │
  3418→│                                                     │
  3419→│ Rank │ Train        │ Current Km │ Delta │ Status  │
  3420→│ ─────┼──────────────┼────────────┼───────┼─────────│
  3421→│  1   │ UTE-2200-015 │   33,750   │ -25%  │ 🔴 HIGH │
  3422→│  2   │ UTE-3400-012 │   39,600   │ -12%  │ 🟡 MED  │
  3423→│  3   │ UTE-2200-008 │   40,200   │ -10%  │ 🟢 LOW  │
  3424→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  3425→```
  3426→
  3427→**Delta Calculation**
  3428→
  3429→```typescript
  3430→interface TrainDelta {
  3431→  trainId: string;
  3432→  trainCode: string;
  3433→  currentKm: number;
  3434→  fleetAverage: number;
  3435→  deltaKm: number;         // currentKm - fleetAverage
  3436→  deltaPercent: number;    // (deltaKm / fleetAverage) * 100
  3437→  severity: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low';
  3438→}
  3439→
  3440→function calculateSeverity(deltaPercent: number): 'high' | 'medium' | 'low' {
  3441→  const absPercent = Math.abs(deltaPercent);
  3442→  if (absPercent > 20) return 'high';
  3443→  if (absPercent > 10) return 'low';
  3444→  return 'low';
  3445→}
  3446→```
  3447→
  3448→**Severity Thresholds**
  3449→
  3450→| Severity | Delta Range | Visual | Action |
  3451→|----------|-------------|--------|--------|
  3452→| High | > ±20% | 🔴 Red | Immediate attention |
  3453→| Medium | ±10% to ±20% | 🟡 Yellow | Monitor closely |
  3454→| Low | < ±10% | 🟢 Green | Acceptable range |
  3455→
  3456→#### 14.3 Outlier Identification
  3457→
  3458→**Outlier Definition**
  3459→
  3460→Statistical outliers: trains with km > 2 standard deviations from mean
  3461→
  3462→**Outlier Panel**

  3465→┌─ Outliers Requiring Attention ──────────────────────┐

  3469→│    UTE-3400-031 (+17%)                              │
  3470→│                                                     │
  3471→│ ⚠️ 2 trains significantly under-running:           │
  3472→│    UTE-2200-015 (-25%), UTE-3400-012 (-12%)        │
  3473→│                                                     │

  3479→```
  3480→
  3481→**Outlier API**
  3482→
  3483→```typescript
  3484→// GET /api/balancing/outliers
  3485→interface OutliersResponse {
  3486→  overRunning: TrainDelta[];
  3487→  underRunning: TrainDelta[];

  3489→    mean: number;

  3493→  };
  3494→}
  3495→```
  3496→
  3497→#### 14.4 Scenario Creation
  3498→
  3499→**Scenario Creation Flow**
  3500→
  3501→```
  3502→1. User clicks "New Scenario"
  3503→2. System copies current state as baseline
  3504→3. User makes adjustments (allocation changes)
  3505→4. User clicks "Run Simulation"
  3506→5. System projects 30-day impact
  3507→6. User views results
  3508→7. User decides: Apply or Discard
  3509→```
  3510→
  3511→**Scenario Data Model**
  3512→
  3513→```typescript
  3514→interface Scenario {
  3515→  id: string;
  3516→  name: string;
  3517→  description?: string;
  3518→  baselineDate: Date;
  3519→  status: 'draft' | 'simulated' | 'applied' | 'discarded';
  3520→  adjustments: ScenarioAdjustment[];
  3521→  results?: SimulationResults;
  3522→  createdAt: Date;
  3523→  createdBy: string;
  3524→}
  3525→
  3526→interface ScenarioAdjustment {
  3527→  type: 'allocation_change' | 'maintenance_reschedule';
  3528→  trainId: string;
  3529→  serviceId?: string;
  3530→  newDate?: Date;
  3531→  description: string;
  3532→}
  3533→```
  3534→
  3535→**Scenario Creation UI**
  3536→
  3537→```
  3538→┌─ New Scenario ──────────────────────────────────────┐
  3539→│                                                     │
  3540→│ Name: [Prioritize Under-runners            ]        │
  3541→│                                                     │
  3542→│ Description:                                        │
  3543→│ [Allocate under-running trains to high-km      ]   │
  3544→│ [services for the next 2 weeks                 ]   │
  3545→│                                                     │
  3546→│ Adjustments:                                        │
  3547→│ ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
  3548→│ │ + UTE-2200-015 → IC 501, IC 505, IC 509      │  │
  3549→│ │ + UTE-3400-012 → IC 502, IC 506, IC 510      │  │
  3550→│ │ - UTE-3400-025 → R 115, R 118 (lower km)     │  │
  3551→│ └───────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
  3552→│                                                     │
  3553→│ [Add Adjustment]  [Import from Solver]              │
  3554→│                                                     │
  3555→│              [Cancel]  [Save Draft]  [Run Simulation]│
  3556→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  3557→```
  3558→
  3559→#### 14.5 Simulation Execution
  3560→
  3561→**Simulation Process**
  3562→
  3563→```
  3564→POST /api/scenarios/{id}/simulate
  3565→
  3566→1. Load scenario adjustments
  3567→2. Project train km for next 30 days
  3568→3. Calculate impact metrics
  3569→4. Compare with baseline
  3570→5. Return results
  3571→```
  3572→
  3573→**Simulation Results**
  3574→
  3575→```typescript
  3576→interface SimulationResults {
  3577→  projectionDays: number;
  3578→  baseline: FleetMetrics;
  3579→  projected: FleetMetrics;
  3580→  impact: {
  3581→    varianceChange: number;      // -4.3% (variance reduced)
  3582→    deadRunningChange: number;   // +3.2% (dead running increased)
  3583→    overRunnerCount: number;
  3584→    underRunnerCount: number;
  3585→  };
  3586→  trainProjections: TrainProjection[];
  3587→}
  3588→
  3589→interface FleetMetrics {
  3590→  averageKm: number;
  3591→  variance: number;
  3592→  standardDeviation: number;
  3593→  totalDeadRunning: number;
  3594→}
  3595→
  3596→interface TrainProjection {
  3597→  trainId: string;
  3598→  currentKm: number;
  3599→  projectedKm: number;
  3600→  currentDelta: number;
  3601→  projectedDelta: number;
  3602→}
  3603→```
  3604→
  3605→**Loading State**
  3606→
  3607→During simulation (typically 5-10 seconds):
  3608→
  3609→```
  3610→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  3611→│                                                     │
  3612→│         ◐ Running simulation...                     │
  3613→│                                                     │
  3614→│         Projecting 30 days for 112 trains          │
  3615→│         Calculating km impact                       │
  3616→│         Analyzing fleet distribution                │
  3617→│                                                     │
  3618→│         Elapsed: 3.2s                               │
  3619→│                                                     │
  3620→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  3621→```
  3622→
  3623→#### 14.6 Scenario Comparison
  3624→
  3625→**Side-by-Side View**
  3626→
  3627→```
  3628→┌─ Scenario Comparison ───────────────────────────────┐
  3629→│                                                     │
  3630→│    Baseline (Current)    │   Prioritize Under-run  │
  3631→│ ─────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────│
  3632→│                          │                         │
  3633→│    Variance: 12.3%       │   Variance: 8.0%        │
  3634→│    ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓░░░░░░   │   ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓░░░░░░░░░░   │
  3635→│                          │        ↓ -4.3%          │
  3636→│                          │                         │
  3637→│    Dead Running: 245km/d │   Dead Running: 253km/d │
  3638→│                          │        ↑ +3.2%          │
  3639→│                          │                         │
  3640→│    Over-runners: 5       │   Over-runners: 3       │
  3641→│    Under-runners: 4      │   Under-runners: 2      │
  3642→│                          │                         │
  3643→│ ─────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────│
  3644→│                                                     │
  3645→│ Trade-off Analysis:                                 │
  3646→│ ✅ Variance reduction outweighs dead running cost  │

  3649→│                                                     │
  3650→│                    [Discard]  [Apply Scenario]      │
  3651→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  3652→```
  3653→
  3654→**Comparison Metrics**
  3655→
  3656→| Metric | Baseline | Scenario | Change | Verdict |
  3657→|--------|----------|----------|--------|---------|
  3658→| Variance | 12.3% | 8.0% | -4.3pp | ✅ Better |
  3659→| Dead Running | 245 km/day | 253 km/day | +3.2% | ⚠️ Worse |
  3660→| Over-runners | 5 | 3 | -2 | ✅ Better |
  3661→| Under-runners | 4 | 2 | -2 | ✅ Better |
  3662→
  3663→**Apply Scenario**
  3664→
  3665→When user clicks "Apply Scenario":
  3666→
  3667→1. Confirm dialog: "This will apply 6 allocation changes. Continue?"
  3668→2. On confirm: Create allocations in database
  3669→3. Toast: "Scenario applied successfully"
  3670→4. Navigate to allocation list showing new allocations

  3739→
  3740→**Block Data Model**
  3741→
  3742→```typescript
  3743→interface MaintenanceBlock {
  3744→  id: string;
  3745→  trainId: string;
  3746→  trainCode: string;
  3747→  depotId: string;
  3748→  type: 'PM1' | 'PM2' | 'HM' | 'OVH';
  3749→  scheduledDate: Date;
  3750→  durationDays: number;
  3751→  status: 'scheduled' | 'in_progress' | 'completed';
  3752→  canReschedule: boolean;
  3753→}
  3754→```
  3755→
  3756→#### 15.3 Drag-Drop Scheduling
  3757→
  3758→**Drag States**
  3759→
  3760→```typescript
  3761→type DragState =
  3762→  | { type: 'idle' }
  3763→  | { type: 'dragging'; blockId: string; originalDate: Date }
  3764→  | { type: 'hovering'; blockId: string; targetDate: Date; isValid: boolean };
  3765→```
  3766→
  3767→**Drag Visual Feedback**
  3768→
  3769→```
  3770→During drag:
  3771→- Source cell: Dashed outline (ghost)
  3772→- Dragged block: Semi-transparent (opacity: 0.7)
  3773→- Valid drop zone: Green highlight
  3774→- Invalid drop zone: Red highlight + X cursor
  3775→```
  3776→
  3777→**Drop Zone Validation**
  3778→
  3779→```typescript
  3780→interface DropValidation {
  3781→  isValid: boolean;
  3782→  reason?: string;
  3783→}
  3784→
  3785→function validateDrop(block: MaintenanceBlock, targetDate: Date, targetDepot: string): DropValidation {
  3786→  // Check depot capacity
  3787→  const capacity = getDepotCapacity(targetDepot, targetDate);
  3788→  if (capacity.utilized >= capacity.total) {
  3789→    return { isValid: false, reason: 'Depot at full capacity' };
  3790→  }
  3791→
  3792→  // Check maintenance interval
  3793→  const lastMaintenance = getLastMaintenance(block.trainId, block.type);
  3794→  const kmSinceLast = getCurrentKm(block.trainId) - lastMaintenance.km;
  3795→  if (kmSinceLast < getMinInterval(block.type)) {
  3796→    return { isValid: false, reason: 'Too early for this maintenance type' };
  3797→  }
  3798→
  3799→  // Check train availability
  3800→  const allocation = getAllocation(block.trainId, targetDate);
  3801→  if (allocation) {
  3802→    return { isValid: false, reason: `Train allocated to ${allocation.serviceCode}` };
  3803→  }
  3804→
  3805→  return { isValid: true };
  3806→}
  3807→```
  3808→
  3809→**Drag Completion**
  3810→
  3811→On valid drop:
  3812→1. Show confirmation toast: "PM1 for UTE-3400-012 moved to Jan 18"
  3813→2. Update database via PATCH /api/maintenance/{id}
  3814→3. Refresh Gantt view
  3815→4. Update depot capacity indicators
  3816→
  3817→On invalid drop:
  3818→1. Block snaps back to original position
  3819→2. Show error toast with reason
  3820→3. Highlight constraint violation
  3821→
  3822→#### 15.4 Constraint Validation
  3823→
  3824→**Constraint Types**
  3825→
  3826→| Constraint | Validation | Error Message |
  3827→|------------|------------|---------------|
  3828→| Depot Capacity | Max simultaneous maintenances | "Depot {name} at capacity on {date}" |
  3829→| Train Availability | No service allocation conflict | "Train allocated to {service} on {date}" |
  3830→| Maintenance Interval | Minimum km between same type | "{type} interval not yet reached" |
  3831→| Duration Fit | Block fits within selected period | "Maintenance spans beyond view period" |
  3832→
  3833→**Real-Time Validation**
  3834→
  3835→```typescript
  3836→// Validate as user drags
  3837→function onDragOver(blockId: string, targetDate: Date, targetDepot: string) {
  3838→  const validation = validateDrop(
  3839→    getBlock(blockId),
  3840→    targetDate,
  3841→    targetDepot
  3842→  );
  3843→
  3844→  setDropIndicator({
  3845→    date: targetDate,
  3846→    depot: targetDepot,
  3847→    isValid: validation.isValid,
  3848→    message: validation.reason,
  3849→  });
  3850→}
  3851→```
  3852→
  3853→**Constraint Tooltip**
  3854→
  3855→When hovering over invalid drop zone:
  3856→
  3857→```
  3858→┌─────────────────────────────────┐
  3859→│ ❌ Cannot schedule here         │
  3860→│                                 │
  3861→│ Porto depot at full capacity    │
  3862→│ (5/5 tracks scheduled)          │
  3863→│                                 │

  3866→```

  4068→#### 16.4 Service Communication
  4069→
  4070→**Frontend ↔ Backend**
  4071→
  4072→```
  4073→Protocol: HTTP/REST
  4074→Format: JSON
  4075→Authentication: None (demo mode)
  4076→Base URL: http://localhost:4000/api
  4077→
  4078→Request Headers:
  4079→  Content-Type: application/json
  4080→  Accept: application/json
  4081→
  4082→Response Format:
  4083→  Success: { data: T, meta?: object }
  4084→  Error: { error: string, code: string, details?: object }

  4086→
  4087→**Backend ↔ Solver**
  4088→
  4089→```
  4090→Protocol: HTTP/REST
  4091→Format: JSON
  4092→Timeout: 30 seconds
  4093→Base URL: http://localhost:8000
  4094→
  4095→Retry Policy:
  4096→  Max retries: 2
  4097→  Backoff: 500ms, 1000ms
  4098→  Fallback: Simple scoring if solver unavailable

  4100→
  4101→**Backend ↔ Database**
  4102→
  4103→```
  4104→Protocol: PostgreSQL wire protocol
  4105→ORM: Drizzle (type-safe SQL)
  4106→Connection Pool: 10 connections
  4107→Timeout: 5 seconds per query

  4109→
  4110→**Backend ↔ Redis**
  4111→
  4112→```

  4114→Purpose: Caching, session storage
  4115→TTL Strategy:
  4116→  - Dashboard data: 60 seconds
  4117→  - Train list: 30 seconds
  4118→  - Solver results: 300 seconds
  4119→  - Static data: 1 hour
  4120→```
  4121→
  4122→#### 16.5 Key Architectural Decisions
  4123→
  4124→**ADR-001: Next.js App Router over Pages Router**
  4125→
  4126→- **Decision:** Use App Router (app/ directory)
  4127→- **Context:** Next.js 14 offers App Router with React Server Components
  4128→- **Rationale:** Better data fetching, layout nesting, streaming SSR
  4129→- **Consequences:** Some learning curve, but better long-term architecture
  4130→
  4131→**ADR-002: Fastify over Express**
  4132→
  4133→- **Decision:** Use Fastify as API framework
  4134→- **Context:** Need performant, type-safe backend
  4135→- **Rationale:** 2-3x faster than Express, built-in schema validation, TypeScript support
  4136→- **Consequences:** Slightly different plugin system, excellent performance
  4137→
  4138→**ADR-003: @belt/tools over React useState**
  4139→
  4140→- **Decision:** Use @belt/tools slice-based state management for application state
  4141→- **Context:** Complex state with animations, real-time updates, 60fps WebGL rendering
  4142→- **Rationale:** Slice-based architecture with Immer reducers avoids stale closure issues; `trackMetadata: false` option prevents GC thrashing in animation loops; auto-generated async actions simplify data fetching
  4143→- **Consequences:** Learning curve for slice/machine pattern, but eliminates common React bugs and enables 60fps performance
  4144→
  4145→**ADR-004: Blueprint.js over shadcn/ui**
  4146→
  4147→- **Decision:** Use Blueprint.js for UI components
  4148→- **Context:** Need enterprise-grade components with dark mode
  4149→- **Rationale:** Built-in dark mode, table/tree components, consistent design
  4150→- **Consequences:** Larger bundle, but comprehensive component library
  4151→
  4152→**ADR-005: Drizzle ORM over Prisma**
  4153→
  4154→- **Decision:** Use Drizzle for database access
  4155→- **Context:** Need type-safe, performant database layer
  4156→- **Rationale:** SQL-like syntax, better performance, smaller bundle
  4157→- **Consequences:** More manual work, but more control
  4158→
  4159→**ADR-006: Separate Python Solver**
  4160→
  4161→- **Decision:** Run solver as separate service, not Node.js library
  4162→- **Context:** Need OR-Tools for optimization
  4163→- **Rationale:** OR-Tools is Python-native, best performance
  4164→- **Consequences:** Extra service to deploy, HTTP overhead acceptable
  4165→

  4251→// File: components/dashboard/KPICard.tsx
  4252→// Export: Named export matching filename
  4253→export function KPICard(props: KPICardProps) { ... }
  4254→
  4255→// Types co-located with component
  4256→interface KPICardProps {
  4257→  title: string;
  4258→  value: number | string;
  4259→  subtitle?: string;
  4260→  status?: 'green' | 'yellow' | 'red';
  4261→  onClick?: () => void;
  4262→}
  4263→```
  4264→
  4265→**Component Structure Pattern**
  4266→
  4267→```typescript
  4268→// Standard component structure
  4269→import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
  4270→import { Card } from '@blueprintjs/core';
  4271→
  4272→interface Props {
  4273→  // Props interface
  4274→}
  4275→
  4276→export function ComponentName({ prop1, prop2 }: Props) {
  4277→  // 1. Hooks (store selectors, effects)
  4278→  const data = useSelector(selectData);
  4279→
  4280→  // 2. Derived state
  4281→  const computedValue = useMemo(() => ..., [data]);
  4282→
  4283→  // 3. Event handlers
  4284→  const handleClick = useCallback(() => ..., []);
  4285→
  4286→  // 4. Render
  4287→  return (
  4288→    <Card className={cn('...')}>
  4289→      {/* JSX */}
  4290→    </Card>
  4291→  );
  4292→}
  4293→```
  4294→
  4295→**Component Categories**
  4296→
  4297→| Category | Purpose | Example |
  4298→|----------|---------|---------|
  4299→| Layout | Page structure | `Navbar`, `Sidebar` |
  4300→| Container | Data fetching, state | `DashboardContainer` |
  4301→| Presentational | Pure UI | `KPICard`, `TrainMarker` |
  4302→| Interactive | User actions | `ServiceList`, `GanttBlock` |
  4303→| Composite | Multiple components | `RecommendationPanel` |
  4304→
  4305→#### 17.3 State Management (@belt/tools)
  4306→
  4307→MMS uses `@belt/tools` for state management, providing slice-based architecture with Immer reducers optimized for 60fps canvas rendering.
  4308→
  4309→**Overview**
  4310→
  4311→| Concept | Description | Use Case |
  4312→|---------|-------------|----------|
  4313→| **Sync Slice** | UI state with Immer reducers | Viewport, selections, UI toggles |
  4314→| **Async Slice** | Data fetching with auto-generated actions | API calls, loading states |
  4315→| **Machine** | Combines slices with computed selectors | Application-wide state |
  4316→
  4317→**Installation**
  4318→
  4319→```bash
  4320→bun add @belt/tools
  4321→```
  4322→
  4323→**Sync Slice (UI State)**
  4324→
  4325→```typescript
  4326→// slices/viewportSlice.ts
  4327→import { createSlice } from '@belt/tools';
  4328→
  4329→export const viewportSlice = createSlice({
  4330→  id: 'viewport',
  4331→  initial: {
  4332→    camera: { x: 0, y: 0, zoom: 1 },
  4333→    lod: 'MESO' as const,
  4334→    selectedTrainId: null as string | null,
  4335→  },
  4336→  reducers: {
  4337→    setCamera: (state, camera: { x: number; y: number; zoom: number }) => {
  4338→      state.camera = camera;
  4339→      // Compute LOD based on zoom
  4340→      if (camera.zoom < 0.5) state.lod = 'MACRO';
  4341→      else if (camera.zoom > 2.0) state.lod = 'MICRO';
  4342→      else state.lod = 'MESO';
  4343→    },
  4344→    selectTrain: (state, trainId: string | null) => {
  4345→      state.selectedTrainId = trainId;
  4346→    },
  4347→  },
  4348→  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  4349→});
  4350→```
  4351→
  4352→**Async Slice (Data Fetching)**
  4353→
  4354→```typescript
  4355→// slices/trainsSlice.ts
  4356→import { createAsyncSlice } from '@belt/tools';
  4357→import type { Train } from '../types';
  4358→
  4359→export const trainsSlice = createAsyncSlice({
  4360→  id: 'trains',
  4361→  load: async (_, context): Promise<Train[]> => {
  4362→    const response = await context.api.get('/api/v1/trains');
  4363→    return response.data;
  4364→  },
  4365→  context: {
  4366→    api: {} as ApiClient,
  4367→  },
  4368→  cache: {
  4369→    policy: 'private',
  4370→    ttl: '5m',
  4371→    swr: true, // Stale-while-revalidate
  4372→  },
  4373→});
  4374→
  4375→// Auto-generated actions:
  4376→// - trainsSlice.actions.load(payload)
  4377→// - trainsSlice.actions.success(data)
  4378→// - trainsSlice.actions.fail(error)
  4379→// - trainsSlice.actions.clear()
  4380→```
  4381→
  4382→**Machine (Combining Slices)**
  4383→
  4384→```typescript
  4385→// state/canvasMachine.ts
  4386→import { createMachine } from '@belt/tools';
  4387→import { viewportSlice } from './viewportSlice';
  4388→import { trainsSlice } from './trainsSlice';
  4389→import { tasksSlice } from './tasksSlice';
  4390→
  4391→export const canvasMachine = createMachine({
  4392→  id: 'canvas',
  4393→  slices: {
  4394→    viewport: viewportSlice,
  4395→    trains: trainsSlice,
  4396→    tasks: tasksSlice,
  4397→  },
  4398→  selectors: {
  4399→    // Computed values (memoized)
  4400→    visibleTasks: (state) => {
  4401→      const { trains, tasks, viewport } = state;
  4402→      if (!tasks.value) return [];
  4403→      return filterByViewport(tasks.value, viewport.camera);
  4404→    },
  4405→    hasConflicts: (state) => {
  4406→      return state.tasks.value?.some(t => t.hasConflict) ?? false;
  4407→    },
  4408→    selectedTrain: (state) => {
  4409→      const id = state.viewport.selectedTrainId;
  4410→      return state.trains.value?.find(t => t.id === id);
  4411→    },
  4412→  },
  4413→  // CRITICAL for 60fps rendering - disables metadata tracking
  4414→  trackMetadata: false,
  4415→});
  4416→
  4417→// Generated type-safe hooks:
  4418→// - canvasMachine.useCanvas('viewport')  → { state, actions }
  4419→// - canvasMachine.useCanvasSelector(s => s.visibleTasks)
  4420→```
  4421→
  4422→**Usage in Components**
  4423→
  4424→```typescript
  4425→// components/canvas/CanvasControls.tsx
  4426→import { canvasMachine } from '../state/canvasMachine';
  4427→
  4428→function CanvasControls() {
  4429→  // Access slice state and actions
  4430→  const { state: viewport, actions } = canvasMachine.useCanvas('viewport');
  4431→
  4432→  // Or use selector for computed values
  4433→  const visibleTasks = canvasMachine.useCanvasSelector(s => s.visibleTasks);
  4434→  const hasConflicts = canvasMachine.useCanvasSelector(s => s.hasConflicts);
  4435→
  4436→  const handleZoom = (delta: number) => {
  4437→    const newZoom = viewport.camera.zoom + delta;
  4438→    actions.setCamera({
  4439→      ...viewport.camera,
  4440→      zoom: Math.max(0.1, Math.min(5, newZoom)),
  4441→    });
  4442→  };
  4443→
  4444→  return (
  4445→    <div className="canvas-controls">
  4446→      <span>Zoom: {viewport.camera.zoom.toFixed(2)}x</span>
  4447→      <span>LOD: {viewport.lod}</span>
  4448→      <span>Tasks: {visibleTasks.length}</span>
  4449→      {hasConflicts && <Tag intent="danger">Conflicts</Tag>}
  4450→      <Button onClick={() => handleZoom(0.1)} icon="plus" />
  4451→      <Button onClick={() => handleZoom(-0.1)} icon="minus" />
  4452→    </div>
  4453→  );
  4454→}
  4455→```
  4456→
  4457→**Cache Policies**
  4458→
  4459→| Policy | Scope | Invalidation | Use Case |
  4460→|--------|-------|--------------|----------|
  4461→| `public` | Global | TTL only | Feature flags, config |
  4462→| `private` | User | Login/logout or TTL | User preferences, permissions |
  4463→| `session` | App | App close or TTL | Viewport state, UI selections |
  4464→
  4465→**Performance: trackMetadata**
  4466→
  4467→For 60fps applications (WebGL, games, animations):
  4468→
  4469→```typescript
  4470→createMachine({
  4471→  id: 'canvas',
  4472→  slices: { ... },
  4473→  // Disable metadata tracking to prevent GC thrashing
  4474→  trackMetadata: false,
  4475→});
  4476→```
  4477→
  4478→With `trackMetadata: false`:
  4479→- No object allocation per dispatch
  4480→- No timestamp tracking
  4481→- Suitable for high-frequency updates (pan, zoom, animation frames)
  4482→
  4483→**Key Differences from XState/FSM**
  4484→
  4485→| XState (FSM) | @belt/tools (Slices) |
  4486→|--------------|----------------------|
  4487→| `states: { idle: { on: {...} } }` | `reducers: { action: (state, payload) => {...} }` |
  4488→| `createMachine({ initial, states })` | `createSlice({ id, initial, reducers })` |
  4489→| `send('EVENT')` | `actions.actionName(payload)` |
  4490→| `useActor(machine)` | `machine.useSlice('sliceId')` |
  4491→| Guards and transitions | Simple reducer logic with Immer |
  4492→
  4493→#### 17.4 API Client Pattern
  4494→
  4495→**Centralized API Client**
  4496→
  4497→```typescript
  4498→// lib/api.ts
  4499→const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://localhost:4000/api';

<system-reminder>

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

file.content:
| Priority | Label | Definition | Demo Impact |
|----------|-------|------------|-------------|
| **P0** | Critical | Without this, demo fails | Must work flawlessly |
| **P1** | Important | Significantly enhances impression | Should work if time permits |
| **P2** | Nice-to-Have | Shows polish if present | Implement only if core is solid |

**Priority Assignment Rationale**

```
P0 Decision Tree:
├── Does the client specifically expect this feature?
│   ├── Yes → P0
│   └── No → Continue
├── Does the demo fail without it?
│   ├── Yes → P0
│   └── No → Continue
├── Is it a core differentiator?
│   ├── Yes → P0
│   └── No → P1 or P2

P1 Decision Tree:
├── Is it a market-standard feature?
│   ├── Yes → P1
│   └── No → Continue
├── Does it significantly improve UX?
│   ├── Yes → P1
│   └── No → P2
```

**P0 Feature Summary (12 items)**

1. Dashboard with KPI cards
2. Km variance indicator (traffic light)
3. Map with train positions
4. Depot markers on map
5. Allocation recommendation engine
6. Justification display (3+ reasons)
7. One-click allocation
8. Km distribution visualization
9. Train outlier identification
10. Scenario creation
11. Simulation execution
12. Scenario comparison

**P1 Feature Summary (8 items)**

1. Maintenance Gantt chart
2. Drag-drop scheduling
3. Constraint validation
4. Depot capacity heatmap
5. Train list filtering
6. Train details with history
7. Visual alerts system
8. Maintenance type color coding

#### 10.4 Scope Boundaries

**Explicitly IN Scope**

| Area | Included | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|
| Train Allocation | Single-day allocation workflow | One train to one service |
| Km Balancing | Fleet-level visualization | Histogram, rankings, deltas |
| Simulation | What-if scenario system | Create, run, compare |
| Maintenance View | Read and reschedule | Gantt with drag-drop |
| Map | Current positions | Train and depot markers |
| Data | Demo dataset | 80-120 trains, seeded |

**Explicitly OUT of Scope**

| Area | Excluded | Why | Future Phase |
|------|----------|-----|--------------|
| Multi-Day Optimization | MILP solver for week/month | Complexity exceeds 3 months | Phase 2 |
| Real EAM Integration | SAP PM, Maximo connections | Requires client environment | Phase 2 |
| Disruption Management | Real-time failure handling | Different architecture | Phase 3 |
| Crew Scheduling | Driver allocation | Doubles complexity | Phase 3 |
| Native Mobile | iOS/Android apps | Responsive web sufficient | If demanded |
| Multi-Language | UI translations | English for demo | Production |
| User Authentication | Login, roles, permissions | Demo runs as single user | Production |
| Audit Trail | Change history logging | Not visible in demo | Production |
| Notification System | Email/SMS alerts | Requires infrastructure | Production |

**Scope Control Principle**

> "When in doubt, leave it out. It's better to demo 6 features that work perfectly than 12 features with bugs."

The scope is intentionally constrained to ensure quality within the 12-week timeline. Any scope addition requires removing something of equal effort.

---

### Chapter 11: Module - Dashboard & KPIs

The Dashboard is the entry point to MMS and must create an immediate positive impression while providing actionable information.

#### 11.1 KPI Cards Specification

**Card: Total Fleet**

```
┌─────────────────────────┐
│  Total Fleet            │
│                         │
│       112               │
│       trains            │
│                         │
│  🟢 98 fit  🔴 14 unfit │
└─────────────────────────┘
```

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Data Source | `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM trains` |
| Refresh Rate | Every 60 seconds |
| Click Action | Navigate to Fleet List |
| Sub-metrics | Fit count, unfit count |
| Alert Condition | Unfit > 15% of fleet |

**Card: Km Variance**

```
┌─────────────────────────┐
│  Km Variance            │
│                         │
│       12.3%             │
│    ████████░░ target 10%│
│                         │
│  ↑ 2.1% from last week  │
└─────────────────────────┘
```

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Formula | `stddev(current_km) / avg(current_km) * 100` |
| Refresh Rate | Every 60 seconds |
| Click Action | Navigate to Balancing |
| Threshold | Green ≤10%, Yellow 10-15%, Red >15% |
| Trend | Compare with 7 days ago |

**Card: Pending Allocations**

```
┌─────────────────────────┐
│  Pending Allocations    │
│                         │
│        24               │
│      services           │
│                         │
│  🔴 8 unassigned today  │
└─────────────────────────┘
```

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Data Source | `WHERE status = 'pending' AND date >= TODAY` |
| Refresh Rate | Every 30 seconds |
| Click Action | Navigate to Allocation |
| Alert Condition | Any service unassigned for today |
| Urgency | Services within 4 hours highlighted |

**Card: Maintenance Due**

```
┌─────────────────────────┐
│  Maintenance Due        │
│                         │
│        17               │
│     this week           │
│                         │
│  PM1: 8  PM2: 6  HM: 3  │
└─────────────────────────┘
```

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Data Source | `WHERE scheduled_date BETWEEN NOW AND NOW+7days` |
| Refresh Rate | Every 5 minutes |
| Click Action | Navigate to Maintenance Gantt |
| Breakdown | Count per maintenance type |
| Alert Condition | Overdue maintenances exist |

#### 11.2 Traffic-Light Indicators

Traffic lights provide instant visual status without requiring interpretation.

**Implementation Specification**

```typescript
type TrafficLightStatus = 'green' | 'yellow' | 'red';

interface TrafficLightConfig {
  metric: string;
  greenThreshold: number;
  yellowThreshold: number;
  direction: 'ascending' | 'descending'; // lower is better vs higher is better
}

const TRAFFIC_LIGHT_CONFIGS: TrafficLightConfig[] = [
  {
    metric: 'kmVariance',
    greenThreshold: 10,
    yellowThreshold: 15,
    direction: 'descending', // lower variance is better
  },
  {
    metric: 'fitPercentage',
    greenThreshold: 90,
    yellowThreshold: 80,
    direction: 'ascending', // higher fit % is better
  },
  {
    metric: 'allocationRate',
    greenThreshold: 95,
    yellowThreshold: 85,
    direction: 'ascending', // higher allocation is better
  },
];
```

**Visual Representation**

```
Green (🟢):  Background: #238823, Border: #1a6b1a
Yellow (🟡): Background: #FFBF00, Border: #cc9900
Red (🔴):    Background: #D2222D, Border: #a61b24
```

**Placement Rules**

- Traffic lights appear in the top-right of each KPI card
- Size: 12px diameter circle
- On hover: Show tooltip explaining status
- On click: Navigate to detail view for that metric

#### 11.3 Depot Capacity Visualization

The depot capacity section shows maintenance resource availability.

**Mini Heatmap Display**

```
┌─ Depot Capacity (Next 7 Days) ─────────────────────┐
│                                                     │
│            Mon   Tue   Wed   Thu   Fri   Sat   Sun │
│ Lisbon     ░░░   ░░░   ███   ███   ░░░   ░░░   ░░░ │
│ Porto      ░░░   ███   ░░░   ░░░   ▓▓▓   ░░░   ░░░ │
│ Coimbra    ███   ░░░   ░░░   ░░░   ░░░   ░░░   ░░░ │
│ Braga      ░░░   ░░░   ░░░   ▓▓▓   ▓▓▓   ░░░   ░░░ │
│                                                     │
│ Legend: ░░░ <50%  ▓▓▓ 50-80%  ███ >80%            │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Capacity Calculation**

```typescript
interface DepotCapacity {
  depotId: string;
  date: Date;
  tracksAvailable: number;
  tracksScheduled: number;
  utilizationPercent: number; // (scheduled / available) * 100
}

function getCapacityColor(utilization: number): string {
  if (utilization < 50) return COLORS.LOW;      // Light gray
  if (utilization < 80) return COLORS.MEDIUM;   // Amber
  return COLORS.HIGH;                            // Red
}
```

**Interaction**

| Action | Result |
|--------|--------|
| Hover cell | Tooltip: "Porto - Wednesday: 3/5 tracks scheduled (60%)" |
| Click cell | Navigate to Gantt view filtered to that depot/date |
| Click depot name | Navigate to depot detail page |

#### 11.4 Maintenance Alerts

Critical alerts appear prominently to demand attention.

**Alert Types**

| Type | Trigger | Visual | Priority |
|------|---------|--------|----------|
| Overdue | Maintenance past due date | Red banner | Critical |
| Imminent | Maintenance within 48 hours | Yellow badge | High |
| Conflict | Double-booking detected | Orange toast | High |
| Capacity | Depot over 90% | Yellow indicator | Medium |

**Alert Component Structure**

```
┌─ Alerts (3) ───────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                     │
│ 🔴 OVERDUE: UTE-3400-025 PM1 was due 2 days ago   │
│    [View Train] [Schedule Now]                     │
│                                                     │
│ 🟡 IMMINENT: 5 trains have PM1 due within 48h     │
│    [View All]                                      │
│                                                     │
│ 🟠 CONFLICT: Porto depot overbooked on Jan 15     │
│    [View Gantt]                                    │
│                                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Alert Data Model**

```typescript
interface Alert {
  id: string;
  type: 'overdue' | 'imminent' | 'conflict' | 'capacity';
  severity: 'critical' | 'high' | 'medium' | 'low';
  title: string;
  description: string;
  trainId?: string;
  depotId?: string;
  actionUrl: string;
  actionLabel: string;
  createdAt: Date;
  acknowledged: boolean;
}
```

#### 11.5 Drill-Down Navigation

Every element on the dashboard is clickable and leads to relevant detail.

**Navigation Map**

```
Dashboard Element          →  Destination
─────────────────────────────────────────────
Total Fleet card           →  /fleet
Km Variance card           →  /balancing
Pending Allocations card   →  /allocation
Maintenance Due card       →  /maintenance
Depot capacity cell        →  /maintenance?depot=X&date=Y
Alert item                 →  Context-specific (train/depot/gantt)
Traffic light indicator    →  Metric detail view
```

**URL Parameters Preserved**

When navigating from dashboard, context is passed via query parameters:

```
/balancing?highlight=over-running
/allocation?urgency=today
/maintenance?depot=lisbon&week=current
/fleet?status=unfit
```

**Breadcrumb Pattern**

All detail pages include breadcrumb navigation back to Dashboard:

```
Dashboard > Balancing > Scenario Comparison
```

---

### Chapter 12: Module - Map & Fleet Visualization

The map provides operational awareness and visual proof of fleet tracking capability.

#### 12.1 Map Provider & Configuration

**Recommended Provider: Mapbox GL JS**

| Aspect | Configuration |
|--------|---------------|
| Provider | Mapbox GL JS v3 |
| Style | Dark mode: `mapbox://styles/mapbox/dark-v11` |
| Initial Center | Portugal: `[-8.2245, 40.1356]` |
| Initial Zoom | 7 (country-level view) |
| Min Zoom | 5 |
| Max Zoom | 14 |
| Terrain | None (2D only) |

**Alternative: Leaflet with Dark Tiles**

If Mapbox is unavailable, use Leaflet with CartoDB dark tiles:

```typescript
L.tileLayer(
  'https://{s}.basemaps.cartocdn.com/dark_all/{z}/{x}/{y}{r}.png',
  { attribution: '© CartoDB' }
);
```

**Map Container Requirements**

- Full width of content area
- Minimum height: 500px
- Maximum height: 70vh
- Border radius: 8px
- No scroll inside map (panning only)

#### 12.2 Train Markers & Status Colors

**Marker Design**

```
Train Marker (16x16px SVG):
┌──────┐
│ ▶──  │  (Simplified train icon)
└──────┘
```

**Status Color Mapping**

| Status | Color | Hex Code | Meaning |
|--------|-------|----------|---------|
| Fit (In Service) | Green | `#2D8A4E` | Operating normally |
| Fit (Available) | Blue | `#2196F3` | Ready for allocation |
| Unfit | Red | `#E63946` | Requires maintenance |
| In Maintenance | Orange | `#FF9800` | Currently at depot |

**Marker Implementation**

```typescript
interface TrainMarker {
  trainId: string;
  position: [number, number]; // [lng, lat]
  status: 'in_service' | 'available' | 'unfit' | 'in_maintenance';
  heading?: number; // 0-360 degrees for rotation
  lastUpdate: Date;
}

function getMarkerColor(status: TrainMarker['status']): string {
  const colors = {
    in_service: '#2D8A4E',
    available: '#2196F3',
    unfit: '#E63946',
    in_maintenance: '#FF9800',
  };
  return colors[status];
}
```

**Marker Hover Behavior**

On hover, show abbreviated tooltip:
```
┌─────────────────────┐
│ UTE-3400-012        │
│ 39,600 km | 🟢 Fit  │
└─────────────────────┘
```

#### 12.3 Depot Markers & Capacity Indicators

**Depot Marker Design**

```
Depot Marker (24x24px):
┌─────────┐
│   🔧    │  (Wrench icon in circle)
│  75%    │  (Capacity text)
└─────────┘
```

**Capacity Ring Visualization**

Each depot marker has a ring showing utilization:

```typescript
interface DepotMarker {
  depotId: string;
  name: string;
  position: [number, number];
  capacityTracks: number;
  scheduledTracks: number;
  utilizationPercent: number;
}

function getCapacityRingColor(utilization: number): string {
  if (utilization < 60) return '#2D8A4E';  // Green
  if (utilization < 90) return '#FF9800';  // Orange
  return '#E63946';                         // Red
}
```

**Depot List Panel**

Below the map, show depot summary table:

```
┌─ Depots ───────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Name        │ Capacity │ Today │ This Week │ Status│
├─────────────┼──────────┼───────┼───────────┼───────┤
│ Lisbon      │ 8 tracks │ 6/8   │ 28/40     │ 🟡 75%│
│ Porto       │ 6 tracks │ 2/6   │ 15/30     │ 🟢 50%│
│ Coimbra     │ 4 tracks │ 4/4   │ 18/20     │ 🔴 90%│
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### 12.4 Train Selection & Popup Cards

**Selection States**

```typescript
type SelectionState = 'none' | 'hover' | 'selected';

interface MapState {

#### 12.5 Auto-Refresh Mechanism

**Refresh Strategy**

| Data | Refresh Interval | Method |
|------|-----------------|--------|
| Train positions | 30 seconds | Polling API |
| Depot capacity | 5 minutes | Polling API |
| Alerts | 30 seconds | Polling API |

**Implementation**

```typescript
// Auto-refresh with visibility check
useEffect(() => {
  if (!document.hidden) {
    const interval = setInterval(() => {
      refreshTrainPositions();
    }, 30_000);
    return () => clearInterval(interval);
  }
}, []);

// Pause refresh when tab is hidden
document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', () => {
  if (document.hidden) {
    pauseRefresh();
  } else {
    resumeRefresh();
  }
});
```

**Loading States**

During refresh, show subtle loading indicator (spinner in corner), not full-page loader.

#### 13.2 Recommendation Engine Integration

**Request Flow**

```
User selects service
        ↓
Frontend calls POST /api/recommend
        ↓
API forwards to Python solver
        ↓
Solver returns ranked list with justifications
        ↓
Frontend displays recommendations
```

**Recommendation API**

```typescript
// Request
interface RecommendRequest {
  serviceId: string;
  date: string;  // ISO date
  limit?: number; // Max recommendations (default 5)
}

// Response
interface RecommendResponse {
  recommendations: Recommendation[];
  computeTimeMs: number;
}

interface Recommendation {
  trainId: string;
  trainCode: string;        // UTE-3400-012
  score: number;            // 0-100
  justifications: Justification[];
  warnings?: string[];
}

interface Justification {
  type: 'km_balance' | 'depot_proximity' | 'maintenance' | 'aptitude' | 'dead_running';
  description: string;
  impact: 'positive' | 'neutral' | 'negative';
  weight: number;           // Contribution to score
}
```

**Solver Scoring Factors**

| Factor | Weight | Description |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| Km Balance | 40% | Distance from fleet average |
| Depot Proximity | 25% | End position near maintenance depot |
| Maintenance Window | 20% | Alignment with scheduled maintenance |
| Dead Running | 15% | Empty km to reach service start |

```

**Justification Icons**

| Icon | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| ✅ | Positive factor (contributes to high score) |
| ⚠️ | Trade-off or warning (not deal-breaker) |
| ❌ | Negative factor (rarely shown for recommendations) |

**Minimum Justifications**

Every recommendation MUST display at least 3 justifications. If the solver produces fewer, the system must generate default explanations based on the scoring factors.

**Delta Calculation**

```typescript
interface TrainDelta {
  trainId: string;
  trainCode: string;
  currentKm: number;
  fleetAverage: number;
  deltaKm: number;         // currentKm - fleetAverage
  deltaPercent: number;    // (deltaKm / fleetAverage) * 100
  severity: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low';
}

function calculateSeverity(deltaPercent: number): 'high' | 'medium' | 'low' {
  const absPercent = Math.abs(deltaPercent);
  if (absPercent > 20) return 'high';
  if (absPercent > 10) return 'low';
  return 'low';
}
```

**Severity Thresholds**

| Severity | Delta Range | Visual | Action |
|----------|-------------|--------|--------|
| High | > ±20% | 🔴 Red | Immediate attention |
| Medium | ±10% to ±20% | 🟡 Yellow | Monitor closely |
| Low | < ±10% | 🟢 Green | Acceptable range |

#### 15.3 Drag-Drop Scheduling

**Drag States**

```typescript
type DragState =
  | { type: 'idle' }
  | { type: 'dragging'; blockId: string; originalDate: Date }
  | { type: 'hovering'; blockId: string; targetDate: Date; isValid: boolean };
```

**Drag Visual Feedback**

```
During drag:
- Source cell: Dashed outline (ghost)
- Dragged block: Semi-transparent (opacity: 0.7)
- Valid drop zone: Green highlight
- Invalid drop zone: Red highlight + X cursor
```

**Drop Zone Validation**

```typescript
interface DropValidation {
  isValid: boolean;
  reason?: string;
}

function validateDrop(block: MaintenanceBlock, targetDate: Date, targetDepot: string): DropValidation {
  // Check depot capacity
  const capacity = getDepotCapacity(targetDepot, targetDate);
  if (capacity.utilized >= capacity.total) {
    return { isValid: false, reason: 'Depot at full capacity' };
  }

  // Check maintenance interval
  const lastMaintenance = getLastMaintenance(block.trainId, block.type);
  const kmSinceLast = getCurrentKm(block.trainId) - lastMaintenance.km;
  if (kmSinceLast < getMinInterval(block.type)) {
    return { isValid: false, reason: 'Too early for this maintenance type' };
  }

  // Check train availability
  const allocation = getAllocation(block.trainId, targetDate);
  if (allocation) {
    return { isValid: false, reason: `Train allocated to ${allocation.serviceCode}` };
  }

  return { isValid: true };
}
```

**Drag Completion**

On valid drop:
1. Show confirmation toast: "PM1 for UTE-3400-012 moved to Jan 18"
2. Update database via PATCH /api/maintenance/{id}
3. Refresh Gantt view
4. Update depot capacity indicators

On invalid drop:
1. Block snaps back to original position
2. Show error toast with reason
3. Highlight constraint violation

#### 15.4 Constraint Validation

**Constraint Types**

| Constraint | Validation | Error Message |
|------------|------------|---------------|
| Depot Capacity | Max simultaneous maintenances | "Depot {name} at capacity on {date}" |
| Train Availability | No service allocation conflict | "Train allocated to {service} on {date}" |
| Maintenance Interval | Minimum km between same type | "{type} interval not yet reached" |
| Duration Fit | Block fits within selected period | "Maintenance spans beyond view period" |

**Real-Time Validation**

```typescript
// Validate as user drags
function onDragOver(blockId: string, targetDate: Date, targetDepot: string) {
  const validation = validateDrop(
    getBlock(blockId),
    targetDate,
    targetDepot
  );

  setDropIndicator({
    date: targetDate,
    depot: targetDepot,
    isValid: validation.isValid,
    message: validation.reason,
  });
}
```

**Constraint Tooltip**

When hovering over invalid drop zone:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────┐
│ ❌ Cannot schedule here         │
│                                 │
│ Porto depot at full capacity    │

#### 16.4 Service Communication

**Frontend ↔ Backend**

```
Protocol: HTTP/REST
Format: JSON
Authentication: None (demo mode)
Base URL: http://localhost:4000/api

Request Headers:
  Content-Type: application/json
  Accept: application/json

Response Format:
  Success: { data: T, meta?: object }
  Error: { error: string, code: string, details?: object }
```

**Backend ↔ Solver**

```
Protocol: HTTP/REST
Format: JSON
Timeout: 30 seconds
Base URL: http://localhost:8000

Retry Policy:
  Max retries: 2
  Backoff: 500ms, 1000ms
  Fallback: Simple scoring if solver unavailable
```

**Backend ↔ Database**

```
Protocol: PostgreSQL wire protocol
ORM: Drizzle (type-safe SQL)
Connection Pool: 10 connections
Timeout: 5 seconds per query
```

**Backend ↔ Redis**

```
Protocol: Redis protocol
Purpose: Caching, session storage
TTL Strategy:
  - Dashboard data: 60 seconds
  - Train list: 30 seconds
  - Solver results: 300 seconds
  - Static data: 1 hour
```

#### 16.5 Key Architectural Decisions

**ADR-001: Next.js App Router over Pages Router**

- **Decision:** Use App Router (app/ directory)
- **Context:** Next.js 14 offers App Router with React Server Components
- **Rationale:** Better data fetching, layout nesting, streaming SSR
- **Consequences:** Some learning curve, but better long-term architecture

**ADR-002: Fastify over Express**

- **Decision:** Use Fastify as API framework
- **Context:** Need performant, type-safe backend
- **Rationale:** 2-3x faster than Express, built-in schema validation, TypeScript support
- **Consequences:** Slightly different plugin system, excellent performance

**ADR-003: @belt/tools over React useState**

- **Decision:** Use @belt/tools slice-based state management for application state
- **Context:** Complex state with animations, real-time updates, 60fps WebGL rendering
- **Rationale:** Slice-based architecture with Immer reducers avoids stale closure issues; `trackMetadata: false` option prevents GC thrashing in animation loops; auto-generated async actions simplify data fetching
- **Consequences:** Learning curve for slice/machine pattern, but eliminates common React bugs and enables 60fps performance

**ADR-004: Blueprint.js over shadcn/ui**

- **Decision:** Use Blueprint.js for UI components
- **Context:** Need enterprise-grade components with dark mode
- **Rationale:** Built-in dark mode, table/tree components, consistent design
- **Consequences:** Larger bundle, but comprehensive component library

**ADR-005: Drizzle ORM over Prisma**

- **Decision:** Use Drizzle for database access
- **Context:** Need type-safe, performant database layer
- **Rationale:** SQL-like syntax, better performance, smaller bundle
- **Consequences:** More manual work, but more control

**ADR-006: Separate Python Solver**

- **Decision:** Run solver as separate service, not Node.js library
- **Context:** Need OR-Tools for optimization
- **Rationale:** OR-Tools is Python-native, best performance
- **Consequences:** Extra service to deploy, HTTP overhead acceptable

#### 17.2 Component Organization

**Component Naming Convention**

```typescript
// File: components/dashboard/KPICard.tsx

export function KPICard(props: KPICardProps) { ... }

}
```

**Component Structure Pattern**

```typescript
// Standard component structure
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
import { Card } from '@blueprintjs/core';

interface Props {
  // Props interface
}

export function ComponentName({ prop1, prop2 }: Props) {
  // 1. Hooks (store selectors, effects)
  const data = useSelector(selectData);

  // 2. Derived state
  const computedValue = useMemo(() => ..., [data]);

  // 3. Event handlers
  const handleClick = useCallback(() => ..., []);

  // 4. Render
  return (
    <Card className={cn('...')}>
      {/* JSX */}
    </Card>
  );
}
```

**Component Categories**

| Category | Purpose | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| Layout | Page structure | `Navbar`, `Sidebar` |
| Container | Data fetching, state | `DashboardContainer` |
| Presentational | Pure UI | `KPICard`, `TrainMarker` |
| Interactive | User actions | `ServiceList`, `GanttBlock` |
| Composite | Multiple components | `RecommendationPanel` |

#### 17.3 State Management (@belt/tools)

MMS uses `@belt/tools` for state management, providing slice-based architecture with Immer reducers optimized for 60fps canvas rendering.

**Overview**

| Concept | Description | Use Case |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| **Sync Slice** | UI state with Immer reducers | Viewport, selections, UI toggles |
| **Async Slice** | Data fetching with auto-generated actions | API calls, loading states |
| **Machine** | Combines slices with computed selectors | Application-wide state |

**Installation**

```bash
bun add @belt/tools
```

**Sync Slice (UI State)**

```typescript
// slices/viewportSlice.ts
import { createSlice } from '@belt/tools';

export const viewportSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'viewport',
  initial: {
    camera: { x: 0, y: 0, zoom: 1 },
    lod: 'MESO' as const,
    selectedTrainId: null as string | null,
  },
  reducers: {
    setCamera: (state, camera: { x: number; y: number; zoom: number }) => {
      state.camera = camera;
      // Compute LOD based on zoom
      if (camera.zoom < 0.5) state.lod = 'MACRO';
      else if (camera.zoom > 2.0) state.lod = 'MICRO';
      else state.lod = 'MESO';
    },
    selectTrain: (state, trainId: string | null) => {
      state.selectedTrainId = trainId;
    },
  },
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
});
```

**Async Slice (Data Fetching)**

```typescript
// slices/trainsSlice.ts
import { createAsyncSlice } from '@belt/tools';
import type { Train } from '../types';

export const trainsSlice = createAsyncSlice({
  id: 'trains',
  load: async (_, context): Promise<Train[]> => {
    const response = await context.api.get('/api/v1/trains');
    return response.data;
  },
  context: {
    api: {} as ApiClient,
  },
  cache: {
    policy: 'private',
    ttl: '5m',
    swr: true, // Stale-while-revalidate
  },
});

// Auto-generated actions:
// - trainsSlice.actions.load(payload)
// - trainsSlice.actions.success(data)
// - trainsSlice.actions.fail(error)
// - trainsSlice.actions.clear()
```

**Machine (Combining Slices)**

```typescript
// state/canvasMachine.ts
import { createMachine } from '@belt/tools';
import { viewportSlice } from './viewportSlice';
import { trainsSlice } from './trainsSlice';
import { tasksSlice } from './tasksSlice';

export const canvasMachine = createMachine({
  id: 'canvas',
  slices: {
    viewport: viewportSlice,
    trains: trainsSlice,
    tasks: tasksSlice,
  },
  selectors: {
    // Computed values (memoized)
    visibleTasks: (state) => {
      const { trains, tasks, viewport } = state;
      if (!tasks.value) return [];
      return filterByViewport(tasks.value, viewport.camera);
    },
    hasConflicts: (state) => {
      return state.tasks.value?.some(t => t.hasConflict) ?? false;
    },
    selectedTrain: (state) => {
      const id = state.viewport.selectedTrainId;
      return state.trains.value?.find(t => t.id === id);
    },
  },
  // CRITICAL for 60fps rendering - disables metadata tracking
  trackMetadata: false,
});

// Generated type-safe hooks:
// - canvasMachine.useCanvas('viewport')  → { state, actions }
// - canvasMachine.useCanvasSelector(s => s.visibleTasks)
```

**Usage in Components**

```typescript
// components/canvas/CanvasControls.tsx
import { canvasMachine } from '../state/canvasMachine';

function CanvasControls() {
  // Access slice state and actions
  const { state: viewport, actions } = canvasMachine.useCanvas('viewport');

  // Or use selector for computed values
  const visibleTasks = canvasMachine.useCanvasSelector(s => s.visibleTasks);
  const hasConflicts = canvasMachine.useCanvasSelector(s => s.hasConflicts);

  const handleZoom = (delta: number) => {
    const newZoom = viewport.camera.zoom + delta;
    actions.setCamera({
      ...viewport.camera,
      zoom: Math.max(0.1, Math.min(5, newZoom)),
    });
  };

  return (
    <div className="canvas-controls">
      <span>Zoom: {viewport.camera.zoom.toFixed(2)}x</span>
      <span>LOD: {viewport.lod}</span>
      <span>Tasks: {visibleTasks.length}</span>
      {hasConflicts && <Tag intent="danger">Conflicts</Tag>}
      <Button onClick={() => handleZoom(0.1)} icon="plus" />
      <Button onClick={() => handleZoom(-0.1)} icon="minus" />
    </div>
  );
}
```

**Cache Policies**

| Policy | Scope | Invalidation | Use Case |
|--------|-------|--------------|----------|
| `public` | Global | TTL only | Feature flags, config |
| `private` | User | Login/logout or TTL | User preferences, permissions |
| `session` | App | App close or TTL | Viewport state, UI selections |

**Performance: trackMetadata**

For 60fps applications (WebGL, games, animations):

```typescript
createMachine({
  id: 'canvas',
  slices: { ... },
  // Disable metadata tracking to prevent GC thrashing
  trackMetadata: false,
});
```

With `trackMetadata: false`:
- No object allocation per dispatch
- No timestamp tracking
- Suitable for high-frequency updates (pan, zoom, animation frames)

**Key Differences from XState/FSM**

| XState (FSM) | @belt/tools (Slices) |
|--------------|----------------------|
| `states: { idle: { on: {...} } }` | `reducers: { action: (state, payload) => {...} }` |
| `createMachine({ initial, states })` | `createSlice({ id, initial, reducers })` |
| `send('EVENT')` | `actions.actionName(payload)` |
| `useActor(machine)` | `machine.useSlice('sliceId')` |
| Guards and transitions | Simple reducer logic with Immer |

#### 17.4 API Client Pattern

**Centralized API Client**

```typescript
// lib/api.ts
const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://localhost:4000/api';

   338→---
   339→
   340→# PART I: BUSINESS CONTEXT & STRATEGY
   341→
   342→---
   343→
   344→## Chapter 1: Executive Summary
   345→
   346→### 1.1 Project Overview
   347→
   348→**MMS (Metro Mileage Management System)** is an enterprise solution designed to centralize and automate mileage management for mixed rolling stock fleets exceeding 100 units. The system bridges the gap between Operations and Maintenance departments through predictive, data-driven planning.
   349→
   350→**Core Problem Statement:**
   351→
   352→Railway operators managing large fleets face three critical pain points:
   353→
   354→1. **Over-running trains** - Units exceeding target mileage degrade faster, increasing maintenance costs and reducing asset lifespan
   355→2. **Under-running trains** - Units falling below target mileage represent idle capital with poor return on investment
   356→3. **Manual planning limitations** - Excel-based planning does not scale for 100+ train fleets and cannot optimize across multiple constraints
   357→
   358→**Solution Vision:**
   359→
   360→MMS addresses all three pain points through:
   361→- Intelligent mileage balancing across the entire fleet
   362→- AI-powered train recommendations with visible justifications
   363→- Predictive maintenance scheduling coordinated with operations
   364→- What-if simulation for strategic planning
   365→
   366→### 1.2 Business Objective
   367→
   368→**Primary Goal:** Win a competitive bid for a railway fleet management contract through a compelling 15-minute demo presentation.
   369→
   370→**Target Audience:**
   371→- Operations Director (decision maker)
   372→- Client's CTO (technical validator)
   373→- Maintenance leadership (domain experts)
   374→
   375→**Success Statement:**
   376→
   377→> After 15 minutes of demo, the client should think: **"They understand our problem better than the others."**
   378→
   379→**Key Differentiators from Competitors:**
   380→
   381→| Differentiator | Why It Matters |
   382→|----------------|----------------|
   383→| Km balancing as PRIMARY objective | Competitors treat this as a secondary feature |
   384→| Unified Maintenance ↔ Operations interface | Eliminates departmental silos, reduces rework |
   385→| Visible dead running cost | Hidden cost made explicit and optimizable |
   386→| Accessible what-if simulation | Decision-makers can "play" with scenarios |
   387→| Transparent decision justification | Trust building through explainability, not black box AI |
   388→
   389→### 1.3 Success Metrics
   390→
   391→**User Success Metrics:**
   392→
   393→| User Role | Success Indicator | Measurement |
   394→|-----------|-------------------|-------------|
   395→| Planner | Visualize fleet km distribution in seconds | < 5s to see complete distribution |
   396→| Planner | Receive justified train recommendation | 3+ reasons per recommendation |
   397→| Planner | Simulate scenarios before deciding | Side-by-side comparison in < 10s |
   398→| Dispatcher | Allocate train to service quickly | < 2 minutes, < 5 clicks |
   399→| Dispatcher | See current train positions on map | Real-time or < 30s refresh |
   400→| Manager | Executive dashboard view | All KPIs visible without navigation |
   401→| All Users | Trust the system recommendations | Decision justification always visible |
   402→
   403→**Business Success Metrics:**
   404→
   405→| Metric | Target |
   406→|--------|--------|
   407→| Demo Duration | 15 minutes maximum |
   408→| Demo Stability | 3 consecutive rehearsals without failures |
   409→| Domain Terminology | 100% correct EN 15380 railway terms |
   410→| Client Reaction | "They understand our problem better than the others" |
   411→
   412→**Technical Success Metrics:**
   413→
   414→| Requirement | Target | Rationale |
   415→|-------------|--------|-----------|
   416→| Allocation Time | < 30 seconds for 100 trains | Dispatcher won't wait longer |
   417→| Screen Loading | < 3 seconds | Credibility in live demo |
   418→| Map Update | < 5 seconds | "Real-time" perception |
   419→| Simulation Response | < 10 seconds | Maintain interactive feel |
   420→| Demo Stability | No crashes for 15 minutes | Critical for presentation |
   421→
   422→### 1.4 Timeline & Constraints
   423→
   424→**Project Timeline:** 12 weeks
   425→
   426→**Milestone Schedule:**
   427→
   428→| Week | Milestone | Deliverable |
   429→|------|-----------|-------------|
   430→| Week 4 | Core solver working | Proof of technical capability |
   431→| Week 6 | Navigable UI with mocked data | Visual credibility established |
   432→| Week 8 | End-to-end flow working | Integration proof complete |
   433→| Week 10 | Specialist validation passed | Domain credibility confirmed |
   434→| Week 12 | Demo-ready with fallbacks | Risk mitigation complete |
   435→
   436→**Technical Constraints:**
   437→
   438→| Constraint | Impact | Mitigation |
   439→|------------|--------|------------|
   440→| 12-week timeline | Limited feature scope | Focus exclusively on P0 features |
   441→| Demo-only scope | No production hardening | Use seed data, skip authentication |
   442→| Python solver requirement | Cross-language integration | JSON REST API between services |
   443→| OR-Tools learning curve | Development complexity | Simplify model, use greedy fallback |
   444→
   445→**Key Assumptions:**
   446→
   447→1. Demo environment runs on stable network (no offline mode needed)
   448→2. Seed data is pre-generated (no real EAM integration)
   449→3. Single-tenant, no multi-organization support required
   450→4. English UI only (no internationalization)
   451→5. Desktop-primary, tablet-secondary (no mobile optimization)
   452→
   453→---
   454→
   455→## Chapter 2: Railway Domain Fundamentals
   456→
   457→### 2.1 Essential Glossary
   458→
   459→All UI labels and system messages MUST use standard EN 15380 railway terminology. Using incorrect terms destroys credibility with railway professionals.
   460→
   461→**Core Terms:**
   462→
   463→| Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |
   464→|------|------------|----------------|
   465→| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance (trains, carriages, locomotives) | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |
   466→| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing the same manufacturer/model and maintenance profile | Group, Class, Collection |
   467→| Depot | Designated maintenance facility for a fleet | Garage, Workshop, Station |
   468→| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness status (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |
   469→| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes/services | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |
   470→| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement of train between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |
   471→| Service | A scheduled revenue-generating journey from origin to destination | Route, Trip, Run |
   472→| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet by controlling km distribution | Usage balancing, Wear distribution |
   473→
   474→**Maintenance Terms:**
   475→
   476→| Term | Definition | Typical Interval |
   477→|------|------------|------------------|
   478→| PM1 | Preventive Maintenance Level 1 (light inspection) | Every 15,000 km |
   479→| PM2 | Preventive Maintenance Level 2 (medium service) | Every 45,000 km |
   480→| HM | Heavy Maintenance (major overhaul) | Every 90,000 km |
   481→| OVH | Overhaul (complete refurbishment) | Every 6 years or 500,000 km |
   482→
   483→**Operational Terms:**
   484→
   485→| Term | Definition |
   486→|------|------------|
   487→| TCO | Total Cost of Ownership over fleet lifecycle |
   488→| Overrun | Train that has exceeded its target mileage |
   489→| Underrun | Train that falls short of its target mileage |
   490→| Variance | Statistical measure of mileage distribution spread across fleet |
   491→| Aptitude | Train's certification for specific line/speed requirements |
   492→
   493→### 2.2 Business Entities & Relationships
   494→
   495→**Entity Relationship Model:**
   496→
   497→```
   498→┌─────────────┐         ┌─────────────┐
   499→│    Fleet    │────────│    Train    │
   500→│             │   1:N  │             │

# PART I: BUSINESS CONTEXT & STRATEGY

---

## Chapter 1: Executive Summary

### 1.1 Project Overview

**MMS (Metro Mileage Management System)** is an enterprise solution designed to centralize and automate mileage management for mixed rolling stock fleets exceeding 100 units. The system bridges the gap between Operations and Maintenance departments through predictive, data-driven planning.

**Core Problem Statement:**

Railway operators managing large fleets face three critical pain points:

1. **Over-running trains** - Units exceeding target mileage degrade faster, increasing maintenance costs and reducing asset lifespan
2. **Under-running trains** - Units falling below target mileage represent idle capital with poor return on investment
3. **Manual planning limitations** - Excel-based planning does not scale for 100+ train fleets and cannot optimize across multiple constraints

**Solution Vision:**

MMS addresses all three pain points through:
- Intelligent mileage balancing across the entire fleet
- AI-powered train recommendations with visible justifications
- Predictive maintenance scheduling coordinated with operations
- What-if simulation for strategic planning

### 1.2 Business Objective

**Primary Goal:** Win a competitive bid for a railway fleet management contract through a compelling 15-minute demo presentation.

**Target Audience:**
- Operations Director (decision maker)
- Client's CTO (technical validator)
- Maintenance leadership (domain experts)

**Success Statement:**

> After 15 minutes of demo, the client should think: **"They understand our problem better than the others."**

**Key Differentiators from Competitors:**

| Differentiator | Why It Matters |
|----------------|----------------|
| Km balancing as PRIMARY objective | Competitors treat this as a secondary feature |
| Unified Maintenance ↔ Operations interface | Eliminates departmental silos, reduces rework |
| Visible dead running cost | Hidden cost made explicit and optimizable |
| Accessible what-if simulation | Decision-makers can "play" with scenarios |
| Transparent decision justification | Trust building through explainability, not black box AI |

### 1.3 Success Metrics

**User Success Metrics:**

| User Role | Success Indicator | Measurement |
|-----------|-------------------|-------------|
| Planner | Visualize fleet km distribution in seconds | < 5s to see complete distribution |
| Planner | Receive justified train recommendation | 3+ reasons per recommendation |
| Planner | Simulate scenarios before deciding | Side-by-side comparison in < 10s |
| Dispatcher | Allocate train to service quickly | < 2 minutes, < 5 clicks |
| Dispatcher | See current train positions on map | Real-time or < 30s refresh |
| Manager | Executive dashboard view | All KPIs visible without navigation |
| All Users | Trust the system recommendations | Decision justification always visible |

**Business Success Metrics:**

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Demo Duration | 15 minutes maximum |
| Demo Stability | 3 consecutive rehearsals without failures |
| Domain Terminology | 100% correct EN 15380 railway terms |
| Client Reaction | "They understand our problem better than the others" |

**Technical Success Metrics:**

| Requirement | Target | Rationale |
|-------------|--------|-----------|
| Allocation Time | < 30 seconds for 100 trains | Dispatcher won't wait longer |
| Screen Loading | < 3 seconds | Credibility in live demo |
| Map Update | < 5 seconds | "Real-time" perception |
| Simulation Response | < 10 seconds | Maintain interactive feel |
| Demo Stability | No crashes for 15 minutes | Critical for presentation |

### 1.4 Timeline & Constraints

**Project Timeline:** 12 weeks

**Milestone Schedule:**

| Week | Milestone | Deliverable |
|------|-----------|-------------|
| Week 4 | Core solver working | Proof of technical capability |
| Week 6 | Navigable UI with mocked data | Visual credibility established |
| Week 8 | End-to-end flow working | Integration proof complete |
| Week 10 | Specialist validation passed | Domain credibility confirmed |
| Week 12 | Demo-ready with fallbacks | Risk mitigation complete |

**Technical Constraints:**

| Constraint | Impact | Mitigation |
|------------|--------|------------|
| 12-week timeline | Limited feature scope | Focus exclusively on P0 features |
| Demo-only scope | No production hardening | Use seed data, skip authentication |
| Python solver requirement | Cross-language integration | JSON REST API between services |
| OR-Tools learning curve | Development complexity | Simplify model, use greedy fallback |

**Key Assumptions:**

1. Demo environment runs on stable network (no offline mode needed)
2. Seed data is pre-generated (no real EAM integration)
3. Single-tenant, no multi-organization support required
4. English UI only (no internationalization)
5. Desktop-primary, tablet-secondary (no mobile optimization)

## Chapter 2: Railway Domain Fundamentals

### 2.1 Essential Glossary

All UI labels and system messages MUST use standard EN 15380 railway terminology. Using incorrect terms destroys credibility with railway professionals.

**Core Terms:**

| Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |
|------|------------|----------------|
| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance (trains, carriages, locomotives) | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |
| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing the same manufacturer/model and maintenance profile | Group, Class, Collection |
| Depot | Designated maintenance facility for a fleet | Garage, Workshop, Station |
| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness status (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |
| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes/services | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |
| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement of train between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |
| Service | A scheduled revenue-generating journey from origin to destination | Route, Trip, Run |
| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet by controlling km distribution | Usage balancing, Wear distribution |

**Maintenance Terms:**

| Term | Definition | Typical Interval |
|------|------------|------------------|
| PM1 | Preventive Maintenance Level 1 (light inspection) | Every 15,000 km |
| PM2 | Preventive Maintenance Level 2 (medium service) | Every 45,000 km |
| HM | Heavy Maintenance (major overhaul) | Every 90,000 km |
| OVH | Overhaul (complete refurbishment) | Every 6 years or 500,000 km |

**Operational Terms:**

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| TCO | Total Cost of Ownership over fleet lifecycle |
| Overrun | Train that has exceeded its target mileage |
| Underrun | Train that falls short of its target mileage |
| Variance | Statistical measure of mileage distribution spread across fleet |
| Aptitude | Train's certification for specific line/speed requirements |

### 2.2 Business Entities & Relationships

**Entity Relationship Model:**

```
┌─────────────┐         ┌─────────────┐
│    Fleet    │────────│    Train    │
│             │   1:N  │             │
